title: cistus ladanifer subsp. angustifolius
intro: Cistus ladanifer subsp. angustifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved rockrose, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub that belongs to the Cistus genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The narrow-leaved rockrose has been found to have various health benefits, including wound healing, reducing inflammation, and preventing infections, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Cistus ladanifer subsp. angustifolius are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
